items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: ALB is currently trading at a forward price-to-sales ratio of 2.34, above the industry's 0.88. Notwithstanding the recent pullback in lithium prices, we advise investors to bet on this Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) stock now, as it has solid earnings growth prospects.

items.headline: UBS Adjusts Price Target on Albemarle to $205 From $264
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $211.44, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB) posted a sharp turnaround. First-quarter 2026 adjusted EBITDA jumped 148% year over year to $664 million as lithium prices rebounded from 2025 lows.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Despite strong fundamental momentum, the stock has recently experienced short-term technical weakness, pulling back to the $140 range, while analysts' average price target is $214.65.

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is likely to continue growing volumes over the coming years from mostly brownfield at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, and opportunities at its Kings Mountain and Antofalla hubs, RBC Capital Markets said. The brokerage said it reiterated its outperform rating on the
